title: Kuzu Tandir Slow Roasted Lamb

seo.title: Kuzu Tandir: Authentic Turkish Slow Roasted Lamb Recipe
seo.metaDescription: Master Kuzu Tandir, the ultimate slow roasted Turkish lamb. This authentic recipe brings the flavors of Turkey to your table. Perfect for a special dinner!
seo.h1: Kuzu Tandir: Authentic Turkish Slow Roasted Lamb Recipe
h2Headings: Ingredients You Need for Kuzu Tandir
Pro Tips for the Best Kuzu Tandir Results
seo.imageAlt: A succulent Kuzu Tandir, traditional Turkish slow roasted lamb, garnished with fresh herbs and ready to serve.
seo.imageCaption: Experience the rich, melt-in-your-mouth tenderness of Kuzu Tandir, a classic Turkish slow-roasted lamb dish perfect for special occasions.

description: Kuzu Tandir is a celebrated Turkish dish featuring lamb slow-roasted to absolute perfection, resulting in incredibly tender, melt-in-your-mouth meat that falls right off the bone. This traditional recipe captures the essence of Turkish hospitality and rich flavors, making it a spectacular centerpiece for any special occasion or family dinner. The simple seasoning allows the natural sweetness and succulence of the lamb to truly shine.

instructions.title: Prepare the Lamb
instructions.text: Preheat your oven to 160°C (325°F). Pat the lamb dry with paper towels. In a small bowl, combine olive oil, salt, black pepper, paprika, and oregano to create a paste. Rub this mixture generously all over the lamb, ensuring it gets into any crevices. If using, make a few shallow cuts on the lamb to help the seasoning penetrate.

instructions.title: Sear the Lamb (Optional)
instructions.text: Heat a large, oven-safe pot or Dutch oven over medium-high heat. Add a little extra olive oil if needed. Sear the lamb on all sides until nicely browned. This step builds flavor and creates a beautiful crust. Remove the lamb from the pot and set aside.

instructions.title: Layer Aromatics
instructions.text: In the same pot, add the chopped onion and smashed garlic. Sauté for 3-5 minutes until softened and fragrant. If using, place the quartered potatoes at the bottom of the pot around the onions and garlic. Return the seared lamb to the pot, resting it on top of the aromatics and potatoes.

instructions.title: Add Liquid and Roast
instructions.text: Pour the water or chicken broth into the pot around the lamb. Add the fresh rosemary or thyme sprigs if desired. Cover the pot tightly with its lid or with aluminum foil. Place the pot in the preheated oven and slow roast for 3 to 3.5 hours, or until the lamb is fork-tender and practically falling off the bone.

instructions.title: Achieve Crispy Skin (Optional)
instructions.text: If you prefer a slightly crispy skin, remove the lid or foil during the last 30 minutes of cooking. Increase the oven temperature to 200°C (390°F) for this final period, monitoring closely to prevent burning.

instructions.title: Rest and Serve
instructions.text: Once cooked, carefully remove the pot from the oven. Transfer the lamb to a large serving platter, tent it loosely with foil, and let it rest for 15-20 minutes. This allows the juices to redistribute, ensuring a more succulent result. Serve the Kuzu Tandir with the roasted potatoes and pan juices.

tips: Always pat the lamb dry before seasoning and searing; this helps create a beautiful crust and allows spices to adhere better.
For an even more aromatic experience, you can add a few bay leaves or whole dried chilis to the pot during the slow roasting process.
To ensure maximum tenderness, do not rush the roasting time. The low and slow cooking method is key to Kuzu Tandir's signature melt-in-your-mouth texture.
article: Kuzu Tandir Slow Roasted Lamb is a beloved Turkish Dinner Recipes dish enjoyed across Turkey. This authentic recipe brings traditional flavors to your kitchen.
